article_title: Roche's Amylin-Based Obesity Drug Petrelintide Posts 10.7 Percent Weight Loss in Phase II but Trails GLP-1 Rivals
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the same topic (PR #193, now published as 09-roches-amylin-based-weight-loss-drug-delivers-107-reduction-in-phase-2)

content_preview.title: Roche's Amylin-Based Obesity Drug Petrelintide Posts 10.7 Percent Weight Loss in Phase II but Trails GLP-1 Rivals
content_preview.summary: Petrelintide achieves placebo-like tolerability with no vomiting at the top dose, but its 9% placebo-adjusted weight loss falls well short of Eli Lilly's competing amylin analog.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Roche and Zealand Pharma announced on March 5 that their jointly developed obesity drug petrelintide met its primary endpoint in a Phase II trial, achieving up to 10.7 percent mean weight loss from baseline at 42 weeks. The drug targets the amylin receptor rather than the GLP-1 pathway that dominates the current obesity market, and demonstrated an unusually clean tolerability profile. However, the magnitude of weight loss fell significantly short of competing therapies, raising ques...

editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE. This article covers the exact same ZUPREME-1 Phase II trial results for petrelintide as PR #193, which was already approved and published as '09-roches-amylin-based-weight-loss-drug-delivers-107-reduction-in-phase-2-but-falls-short-of-eli-lillys-rival-sending-zealand-pharma-shares-down-32'. Same trial data, same competitive comparison with eloralintide, same sources.
